summary: Matched a large portion of src/melee/gm/gmmultiman.c by replacing many placeholder /// # functions with C implementations for Multi-Man mode scene setup, CSS return handling, match-end/result processing, and per-mode score/progress persistence. The PR also tightened several game-mode function prototypes in gm_16F1.h, gm_17C0.c/.h, and gmmultiman.h so callers use concrete return and argument types rather than UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; 426 added lines replacing many placeholder functions with matched Multi-Man mode logic.
Updated gm_801B688C prototype from unknown to bool gm_801B688C(bool).
Refined prototypes used by gmmultiman, including gm_80173460(s8), gm_80173498, gm_80173754(s8, int), and gm_80173C70(s8 c_kind, u16, int, int).
Changed gm_80181A14 return type from u8 to int to match callers/codegen.
Kept gm_80181A14 declaration consistent with the implementation return type change.

what_changed: Implemented gm_801B688C as a bool-returning transition/helper routine that performs card/library calls, checks current mode state with gm_80181A14 and gm_801A4310, updates game-mode records via gm_80173C70/gm_80173EEC/gm_80172898, and decides whether to continue into another Multi-Man-related scene.

Added a static inline helper gm_801B6AD8_inline(MinorScene* scene, int x) shared by several scene setup entry points. It initializes player stocks and scale fields, calls gm_801B06B0, updates lbDvd game_cache character/stage entries, and refreshes DVD cache state.

Implemented multiple wrapper scene setup functions that call gm_801B6AD8_inline with different constants: gm_801B6AD8 uses 0x11, gm_801B7044 uses 0x12, gm_801B75F0 uses 0x13, gm_801B7B74 uses 0x14, gm_801B8110 uses 0x15, and gm_801B863C uses 0x16.

Implemented repeated character-select return handlers such as gm_801B6B70, gm_801B70DC, gm_801B7688, gm_801B7C0C, gm_801B81A8, and gm_801B86D4. These check pending_scene_change == 2, call gm_801A42F8(1) on cancel, reset player data with gm_80167A14, and pass player character/color/xA pointers to gm_801B0730.

Implemented match-start setup and record initialization logic for several Multi-Man variants, including functions around gm_801B6BE8 and existing nearby setup routines. These copy VsModeData into StartMeleeData, apply Multi-Man rules through gm_801B69C0, initialize players, set opposing teams, call gm_8016F088, and update per-character/per-mode records through gm_80181A44, gm_80181AC8, gm_80181B64, and gm_80182DF0.

Implemented match-end handlers gm_801B6F44, gm_801B74F0, gm_801B7AA0, gm_801B8024, gm_801B8580, and gm_801B8AF8. These read MatchExitInfo via gm_801A4284, handle match_end.result == 8 by setting a pending minor scene, record elapsed time and standings, call gm_80182578, persist mode-specific record values through gmMainLib_8015D6A4/D6F8/D74C/D7A4/D7BC/D7D4-family accessors, and call gm_801B688C to decide whether to return to minor scene 0.
Renamed several MinorScene parameters from arg0 to scene in matched functions, making call sites clearer without implying a new symbol name.
Added required includes in gmmultiman.c for lbcardgame.h, lbdvd.h, and lb/types.h while preserving gm headers needed by the new implementations.
Adjusted an unsigned literal/style mismatch in gm_801B7700 by changing temp_r28->x2 = 0U to temp_r28->x2 = 0.
Changed gm_80181A14 from u8-returning to int-returning in gm_17C0.c and gm_17C0.h, consistent with gmmultiman comparisons against zero.
smart_moves: Factored identical preload/setup behavior into static inline gm_801B6AD8_inline rather than duplicating code across six scene setup functions; this likely helped preserve matching while keeping the source readable.
Promoted unknown prototypes to concrete signed/unsigned widths only where call-site evidence supported it, especially s8 character-kind parameters and u16 return/use sites.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in several functions where stack padding was needed, a common matching tactic for this codebase.

Preserved repeated patterns across mode variants instead of over-abstracting all handlers, which is often necessary in decompilation when small differences in record accessors, constants, or casts affect generated assembly.
Kept mode constants explicit, such as 0x21 through 0x26 for different Multi-Man records and 0x11 through 0x16 for setup paths, avoiding unsupported semantic renames.
decomp_lessons: When matching game-mode scene code, first identify repeated minor-scene lifecycle phases: preload/setup, CSS return, match-start data construction, and match-end persistence. Matching one variant can guide nearby variants with different constants and record accessors.
Header prototypes are part of matching work. Incorrect return widths or signedness, such as u8 versus int or int versus s8, can force bad casts or mismatched comparisons in callers.
For character-kind values, this PR provides evidence that s8 is often the correct parameter type in gm_16F1 interfaces, while storage fields may still require explicit u8/s8 casts in edge cases.

Use small static inline helpers when the original code appears to share a codegen pattern. The gm_801B6AD8_inline helper captured common initialization while still allowing constants to vary at wrapper call sites.

Multi-Man match-end functions follow a recognizable persistence pattern: early exit on match_end.result == 8, update elapsed time and standings, flush/update global result state, select the per-character record pointer via gmMainLib_8015Dxxx, write x2/x4 depending on UnkMultimanData state, then decide next scene through gm_801B688C.
The diff contains little human review discussion, so lessons are inferred mainly from the code changes rather than explicit reviewer commentary.
naming_conventions: MinorScene* parameters were consistently named scene in newly matched or touched functions rather than arg0.
Character-kind parameters use c_kind when a semantic name is available, as seen in gm_80173C70(s8 c_kind, u16, int, int).
Temporary variables remain compiler/register-oriented names such as temp_r31, temp_r30, temp_r29, and temp_r3, matching the project's decompilation style for partially understood functions.
New shared helper was named gm_801B6AD8_inline, indicating an inline extracted from or associated with gm_801B6AD8 rather than a newly identified original symbol.
Unknown or partially understood structures remain conservatively named, for example UnkMultimanData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in functions where stack shape required padding for matching.
Used static inline factoring for repeated setup code while preserving wrapper functions at the expected addresses.
Adjusted signedness and casts around c_kind in gm_801B8AF8, including explicit u8, s8, and s32 casts, suggesting careful control of sign extension/codegen.
Changed gm_80181A14 return type to int, likely to align return-value extension and caller comparison codegen.
Changed function signatures in headers to concrete narrow types, such as u8 gm_80173460(s8) and int gm_80173754(s8, int), to make call-site generated code match.
Kept constants and direct global accesses rather than introducing enums or broad abstractions, preserving assembly-sensitive structure.

review_feedback.feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
There was one issue comment counted, but no body text excerpt was provided, so no substantive review feedback can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type UnkMultimanData fields x0_0, x2, and x4.
Search for later cleanup of mode constants 0x21 through 0x26 in Multi-Man record handling.
Inspect later gmmultiman PRs for completion of remaining unmatched functions after pr-1822.
Search for additional prototype corrections to gm_80173C70, gm_80173460, gm_80173498, and gm_80173754.
Look for review or commit history explaining why gm_80181A14 must return int rather than u8.
